product.name: Aramid D1 210 Denim
product.categorySourceGroup: Inherent Flame Retardant Denim and Elasthane Aramid Fabrics
product.titleDescriptor: Aramid Denim Fabric, 93% meta Aramid 5% para Aramid 2% Carbon , 210 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 210 g/m², 93% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, Denim Fabric Aramid denim fabric
descriptions: for electricity distribution companies and oil and gas industry workers.
Inherent flame retardant fabric with EN ISO 11612 and EN ISO 11611 certification.
Protection against thermal hazards of electrical arc. (EN 61482-1-2)
Resistant to most industrial chemicals. (EN ISO 13034)
Aramid Denim Fabric, 93% meta Aramid 5% para Aramid 2% Carbon , 210 gr/m'2
210 g/m², 93% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, Denim Fabric Aramid denim fabric
technicalParameters: Fiber | 93% m-Aramid (m-AR) 5% p-Aramid (p-AR) 2% Carbon(CF) | EN ISO 2076
Tensile Strength | Warp > 1500N; Weft > 1000N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>70N ; Weft>60N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

evidence: for electricity distribution companies and oil and gas industry workers.
Protection against thermal hazards of electrical arc. (EN 61482-1-2)
acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicitly stated intended use for electricity distribution companies with arc-flash protection certification, directly supporting the energy sector application.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: No explicit military, police or tactical use mentioned in the product data.
Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification with 'Welding Protection: Yes' directly supports use in welding/metalworking protective clothing.

evidence: for electricity distribution companies and oil and gas industry workers.
Protection against thermal hazards of electrical arc. (EN 61482-1-2)
primary.rationale: The product explicitly states its target use as electricity distribution companies and oil & gas workers, with certified arc-flash protection (EN 61482), making energy sector the clearest direct application.

evidence: Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612
Resistant to most industrial chemicals. (EN ISO 13034)
secondary.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification independently supports a secondary welding/metallurgy protective clothing use, distinct from the energy-sector arc protection.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: General industrial workwear is too broad given the explicit named electrical/oil & gas and welding sectors.
No military or uniformed-service use stated.
No firefighter-specific use stated; FR properties alone are insufficient.
